summary: Round-2 patch (commit f6a8137) correctly addresses the round-1 security-auditor concern about path-reserved characters in model names (e.g. `litert-community/...`). `Uri.encode(modelName)` on navigate and `Uri.decode(raw)` on read form a symmetric round-trip for the ASCII-safe model names in ModelAllowlist. Only `android.net.Uri` was added to imports; nothing else in SanctumApp.kt was disturbed. AC-mandated call signatures are preserved: `ModelManagerScreen(onLoad = { modelName -> navController.navigate("chat/${Uri.encode(modelName)}") })` with viewModel defaulted and no NavController passed; `ChatScreen(modelName = modelName, onBack = { navController.popBackStack() })` with viewModel defaulted and no NavController passed. The deviation from the task's literal canonical form (adding Uri.encode/decode) is explicitly anticipated in the task's Edge cases section (line 120) and is a net improvement. No regressions, no new findings.
